Knowltonia vesicatoria, commonly known as the bladderpod, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The bladderpod has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Knowltonia vesicatoria include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
